title: Garlicky Syrian Red Potatoes Stir-fry
description: This low-fat-friendly brunch-mood Syrian dish features crispy red potatoes seasoned with garlic and herbs for a savory side or light meal.

ingredients.name: Fresh Herbs (such as Dill or Mint)

instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F (205°C). Wash the red potatoes, trim off any bad spots, and cut them into medium-sized chunks.
Spread the sliced red potatoes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle half of the olive oil over them, toss gently to coat, then season with salt, pepper, and chopped fresh herbs.
Squeeze in lemon juice if using, for extra brightness and freshness. Place the tray in your preheated oven and bake until the potatoes are golden brown and crispy, about 20-30 minutes.
Meanwhile, mince the garlic cloves finely in a small bowl. In another pan over medium heat, pour in the remaining olive oil and add minced garlic when it's hot but not smoking.

Once the garlic is fragrant (about 1 minute), reduce the heat to low and slowly stir-fry until the garlic becomes golden brown and slightly crispy, about 3-5 minutes. Be cautious as the garlic can burn quickly at this stage.
Remove the tray from the oven and toss the baked potatoes with the golden-brown garlic. Serve hot or warm as a side dish or light meal.
